reason: Response A has a more distinctive, risk-taking voice with sharper aphorisms ("only the reader has been revised," "the place where the book hands the world back to you") and a first-person stance that takes an actual position ("I distrust readers who race toward it"). Response B, while polished, leans on more conventional, workshop-style imagery (the door metaphor, "sediment of every other book") and ends with the well-worn "the reading has just started" cliché it even flags as old, reading more like a careful imitation of literary Sonnet prose than the genuine article. The slightly greater willingness in A to commit to an unusual argument rather than survey familiar observations tips me toward it.
